notes: Next.js 15 App Router structure with route groups
API routes: /api/health, /api/seed, /api/stats
Route groups: (chart) for bar-chart, (dashboard) for dashboard
Health check endpoint implemented
Seed endpoint with Prisma/Mirage dual support
Stats endpoint for data retrieval
Comprehensive test coverage for dashboard components
Application layer hooks following Clean Architecture
useDataGeneration: Manages data generation config and state
useStats: SWR-based data fetching with validation
useSeedGeneration: Handles seed data generation
Comprehensive test coverage: config, events, generation, bounds, error handling
Integration tests for data generation flow
Error handling with custom error types
Type-safe API validation with Zod schemas
Dashboard: Main component with data fetching, error handling, notification system
Charts: Comprehensive chart library (DayOfWeek, Emotion, Monthly, Student, TimeOfDay)
DynamicBarChart: Highly configurable with data filtering and edge case handling
Common components: ComponentCard, ErrorBoundary, LoadingSpinner, Notification, Icons
UI components: Button with test coverage
ErrorBoundary with structured logging integration
Comprehensive test coverage: accessibility, dataFetching, interactions, notifications, performance, rendering
Charts have extensive test suites: configuration, dataFiltering, edgeCases, errorHandling, integration
Follows Clean Architecture: imports from schemas, lib (cross-cutting), no domain/infrastructure imports
Domain layer following Clean Architecture (no dependencies on other layers)
Entities: Record (core data model), Stats (statistics aggregate), StatsOverview, MonthlyStats, StudentStats, DayOfWeekStats, TimeOfDayStats
Repositories: StatsRepository (interface), IRecordRepository (interface), MockRecordRepository (implementation for testing)
Services: StatsService (statistics calculation and seed data generation), EmotionGenerator (emotion data generation with patterns)
Data generation: DataGenerationConfig with EMOTION_CONSTANTS and DATA_GENERATION_BOUNDS for realistic emotion simulation
Tests are co-located with source files (domain-specific testing pattern)
Comprehensive test coverage: contract tests, behavior tests, integration tests for repositories
Entity tests: Record validation, Stats aggregation, DataGeneration config/patterns/seasonal effects/events
Clean Architecture compliance: Domain layer has zero imports from infrastructure/presentation/application layers
Infrastructure layer implementing external dependencies (database, services)
PrismaRecordRepository: Full CRUD operations with timeout enforcement (withDatabaseTimeout)
PrismaStatsRepository: Statistics queries with Prisma, all operations wrapped in timeout
PrismaSeedRepository: Seed data generation with database timeout protection
MockStatsRepository: In-memory implementation for testing/Mirage mode
Repository Factory: createStatsRepository() and createStatsService() for environment-based instantiation
DataService: Data generation service with configurable patterns (normal, bimodal, stress, happy), seasonal effects, and event effects
All database operations protected by timeout enforcement (autonomous durability protocol)
Comprehensive test coverage: 13 test files covering CRUD operations, seed generation, configuration, features
Clean Architecture compliance: Imports only from domain and lib (cross-cutting), no imports from presentation/application
Cross-cutting layer with shared utilities and configurations
resilience/: Autonomous durability protocol components (timeout, circuit-breaker, loop-detector, memory-monitor, structured-logger)
All async operations protected by timeout enforcement (SYSTEM_CONSTITUTION.md compliance)
Circuit-breaker pattern for cascading failure prevention
LoopDetector with destroy() method and complete timer cleanup (Jest hang resolved)
StructuredLogger with automatic log compression and visibility filtering
MemoryMonitor for leak detection and GC triggering
api/: API error handling, resilient handler wrapper, request validation, response utilities
withResilientHandler() combines timeout + circuit-breaker + structured logging
config/: Application configuration (APP_CONFIG, EMOTION_CONFIG, UI_CONFIG, CHART_COLORS)
constants/: Centralized constants (messages, UI strings, API config, generation defaults)
error-handler.ts: Type-safe error classes (AppError, ValidationError, NetworkError) with normalization
Comprehensive test coverage: 28 test files covering all resilience patterns, error handling, and configuration
Clean Architecture compliance: Cross-cutting layer, imports allowed from all layers
Recent improvements: Centralized error messages, UI strings, and configuration constants
Presentation layer hook for UI-specific data transformation
useStats: Wrapper around application layer useStats, transforms data for display
Error display formatting using ERROR_MESSAGES constants
Refetch handler with structured logging integration
No dedicated tests (functionality covered by application layer tests)
Follows Clean Architecture: imports from application and lib (cross-cutting)
Minimal presentation logic (delegates to application layer)
Zod-based schema definitions for type-safe API validation
Data generation schemas: DataGenerationConfig with distribution patterns, event effects, class characteristics
API request/response schemas: SeedRequest, SeedResponse, StatsResponse with BaseResponse pattern
Statistics schemas: StatsOverview, MonthlyStats, StudentStats, DayOfWeekStats, TimeOfDayStats
Exported TypeScript types inferred from Zod schemas for full type safety
DEFAULT_CONFIG: Predefined configuration using GENERATION_DEFAULTS from constants
Validation constraints using GENERATION_CONSTRAINTS from lib/constants
Tests co-located with schemas: response validation, config validation, pattern validation
Clean Architecture compliance: Cross-cutting layer, imports only from lib (constants)
3 test files covering schema validation, edge cases, and type inference
Utility functions and helpers for emotion data calculations
statsCalculator.ts: Single file (1010 lines) with comprehensive statistical calculation functions
Core calculations: average, monthly stats, day-of-week stats, time-of-day stats, emotion distribution, student stats, trendline
Emotion generation: generateNormalRandom (Box-Muller transform), generateBaseEmotion with distribution patterns
Seasonal and event effects: calculateSeasonalEffect, calculateEventEffect for realistic emotion simulation
Utility functions: clamp, clampEmotion for value validation
Comprehensive test coverage: 7 test files covering aggregation, basic operations, effects, emotion calculations, stats, time-based calculations, and utilities
Pure functions with no side effects (easy to test and reuse)
Used by domain services for statistics calculation and data generation
Clean Architecture compliance: Imports only from lib (config, constants), cross-cutting utilities
Well-documented with README.md explaining function purposes and usage
